title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: Dataflows identified agency, ID, version. examples guide use IMF Statistics Department agency, IMF.STA.
code: library(imf.data)  datasets <- list_datasets("IMF.STA") datasets[datasets$id == "CPI", ]

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: returned order order used SDMX series key. higher-level workflow, use dimension names named filters list instead assembling positional key.
code: dimensions <- list_dimensions("CPI") dimensions

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: Availability conditional. Adding filters dimensions narrows codes returned requested dimension.
code: list_dimension_values(   "CPI",   "FREQUENCY",   filters = list(COUNTRY = "USA") )

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: result contains one row per observation. SDMX dimensions TIME_PERIOD character columns, OBS_VALUE numeric. Use first_n_obs last_n_obs limit observations per matching series. two arguments combined.
code: cpi <- get_data(   "CPI",   filters = list(     COUNTRY = c("USA", "CAN"),     INDEX_TYPE = "CPI",     COICOP_1999 = "_T",     TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION = "IX",     FREQUENCY = "M"   ),   start_period = "2020",   last_n_obs = 12,   attributes = c("SCALE", "STATUS") ) cpi

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: get_data() returns one row per observation, convenient plotting analysis awkward comparing countries side side. Reshape one column per country base R:
code: wide <- reshape(   cpi[c("COUNTRY", "TIME_PERIOD", "OBS_VALUE")],   idvar = "TIME_PERIOD",   timevar = "COUNTRY",   direction = "wide" ) wide[order(wide$TIME_PERIOD), ]

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: get_data() raw sdmx_*() functions validate arguments contacting API, mistakes raise informative error instead opaque HTTP failure: Use list_dimension_values() confirm code valid current dataflow retrying.
code: tryCatch(   get_data(     "CPI",     filters = list(COUNTRY = "USA"),     first_n_obs = 1,     last_n_obs = 1   ),   error = function(e) conditionMessage(e) )

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: raw functions preserve complete nested SDMX response: Raw JSON responses unsimplified lists zero-based SDMX indexes can decoded without losing structure. XML responses available text setting format = "xml".
code: raw <- sdmx_data(   "CPI",   key = "USA.CPI._T.IX.M",   last_n_obs = 2 )  availability <- sdmx_availability(   "CPI",   component_id = "FREQUENCY",   filters = list(COUNTRY = "USA"),   mode = "available" )  metadata <- sdmx_metadata(detail = "allstubs")

title: Getting started with IMF SDMX 3.0 data
text: httr2 automatically respects standard environment variables HTTPS_PROXY, HTTP_PROXY, NO_PROXY. configure proxy imf.data, use: configuration applies every package request current R session. Remove : Keep credentials environment variables another secrets manager rather writing directly scripts.
code: set_imf_proxy(   "http://proxy.example.com",   port = 8080,   username = Sys.getenv("PROXY_USER"),   password = Sys.getenv("PROXY_PASSWORD") ) clear_imf_proxy()

title: Migrating from imf.data 0.1.x
text: compatibility wrappers old dataset IDs dimension names consistently map new IMF Data Portal.

title: Migrating from imf.data 0.1.x
text: old query created dataset object called generated get_series() method: new workflow first inspects current dimensions, uses SDMX names directly: Important identifier changes include three-letter country codes USA instead previous US, dataset-specific dimension layouts.
code: CPI <- load_datasets("CPI") CPI$get_series(   freq = "M",   ref_area = "US",   indicator = "PCPI_IX" ) list_dimensions("CPI")  get_data(   "CPI",   filters = list(     COUNTRY = "USA",     INDEX_TYPE = "CPI",     COICOP_1999 = "_T",     TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION = "IX",     FREQUENCY = "M"   ) )

title: Migrating from imf.data 0.1.x
text: Find current dataflow list_datasets(). Inspect dimensions list_dimensions(). Validate codes list_dimension_values(). Replace positional generated arguments named filters list. Check returned columns units replacing production results. IMF Data Portal source truth current dataflow definitions vintages.

what: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
title: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
text: imf.data provides R interface IMF SDMX 3.0 API. supports discovering datasets, inspecting dimensions, downloading observations tidy data frames. Raw SDMX responses also available advanced use.

title: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
text: Install imf.data CRAN: Install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("imf.data") pak::pak("pedrobtz/imf.data")

title: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
text: get_data() returns one row per observation. Dimension time columns character vectors, OBS_VALUE numeric, requested SDMX attributes included additional columns.
code: library(imf.data)  # Discover IMF Statistics Department datasets datasets <- list_datasets("IMF.STA") datasets[datasets$id == "CPI", ] # Inspect a dataset's key dimensions list_dimensions("CPI") # Find valid frequency codes for US CPI data list_dimension_values(   "CPI",   "FREQUENCY",   filters = list(COUNTRY = "USA") ) # Download the latest monthly headline CPI observations cpi <- get_data(   "CPI",   filters = list(     COUNTRY = c("USA", "CAN"),     INDEX_TYPE = "CPI",     COICOP_1999 = "_T",     TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION = "IX",     FREQUENCY = "M"   ),   last_n_obs = 12,   attributes = "STATUS" ) cpi

title: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
text: See vignette("getting-started") complete workflow.
code: raw <- sdmx_data(   "CPI",   key = "USA.CPI._T.IX.M",   last_n_obs = 2 )

title: An Interface to IMF (International Monetary Fund) Data
text: httr2 respects standard proxy environment variables HTTPS_PROXY NO_PROXY. proxy can also configured specifically imf.data: package-specific configuration applies raw tidy API functions remainder R session, clear_imf_proxy() called.
code: set_imf_proxy(   "http://proxy.example.com",   port = 8080,   username = Sys.getenv("PROXY_USER"),   password = Sys.getenv("PROXY_PASSWORD") )  list_datasets("IMF.STA") clear_imf_proxy()

what: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
title: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
text: Query dataflow named dimension filters decode indexed SDMX response one row per observation.

title: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
code: get_data(   dataflow,   filters = list(),   agency_id = "IMF.STA",   version = "+",   start_period = NULL,   end_period = NULL,   first_n_obs = NULL,   last_n_obs = NULL,   attributes = character() )

title: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
text: dataflow Dataflow ID. filters Named list dimension selections. agency_id Maintainer ID. version Dataflow version. start_period, end_period Optional SDMX period bounds. first_n_obs, last_n_obs Optional observation limits per series. attributes Character vector series observation attributes include result.

title: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
text: tibble containing dimension columns, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE, requested attributes.

title: Download IMF data as a tidy data frame — get_data
code: # \donttest{ get_data(   "CPI",   filters = list(     COUNTRY = "USA",     INDEX_TYPE = "CPI",     COICOP_1999 = "_T",     TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION = "IX",     FREQUENCY = "M"   ),   last_n_obs = 2 ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   COUNTRY INDEX_TYPE COICOP_1999 TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION FREQUENCY TIME_PERIOD #>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>       <chr>                  <chr>     <chr>       #> 1 USA     CPI        _T          IX                     M         2026-M04    #> 2 USA     CPI        _T          IX                     M         2026-M05    #> # ℹ 1 more variable: OBS_VALUE <dbl> # }

title: List IMF datasets — list_datasets
text: Return available IMF SDMX dataflows stable, tidy catalogue.

title: List IMF datasets — list_datasets
code: list_datasets(agency_id = "*")

title: List IMF datasets — list_datasets
text: agency_id Maintainer ID. Use "*" maintainers.

title: List IMF datasets — list_datasets
text: tibble agency, id, version, name, description, dsd_id columns.

title: List IMF datasets — list_datasets
code: # \donttest{ datasets <- list_datasets("IMF.STA") # }

what: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
title: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
text: Use IMF availability endpoint return codes remain valid dimension, optionally constrained selections dimensions.

title: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
code: list_dimension_values(   dataflow,   dimension,   filters = list(),   agency_id = "IMF.STA",   version = "+" )

title: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
text: dataflow Dataflow ID. dimension Dimension ID. filters Named list selections dimensions. agency_id Maintainer ID. version Dataflow version.

title: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
text: tibble code, name, description columns.

title: List available values for a dataset dimension — list_dimension_values
code: # \donttest{ list_dimension_values(   "CPI",   "FREQUENCY",   filters = list(COUNTRY = "USA") ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   code  name  description #>   <chr> <chr> <chr>       #> 1 A     NA    NA          #> 2 M     NA    NA          #> 3 Q     NA    NA          # }

what: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
title: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
text: Resolve dataflow's data structure return dimensions SDMX key order, including associated codelist one declared.

title: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
code: list_dimensions(dataflow, agency_id = "IMF.STA", version = "+")

title: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
text: dataflow Dataflow ID. agency_id Maintainer ID. version Dataflow version. "+" selects latest stable version.

title: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
text: tibble describing dimension codelist.

title: List the dimensions of an IMF dataset — list_dimensions
code: # \donttest{ list_dimensions("CPI") #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   position id           type  name  codelist_agency codelist_id codelist_version #>      <int> <chr>        <chr> <chr> <chr>           <chr>       <chr>            #> 1        0 COUNTRY      Dime… Coun… IMF             CL_COUNTRY  1.0+.0           #> 2        1 INDEX_TYPE   Dime… Inde… IMF             CL_INDEX_T… 2.0+.0           #> 3        2 COICOP_1999  Dime… Expe… IMF             CL_COICOP_… 1.0+.0           #> 4        3 TYPE_OF_TRA… Dime… Type… IMF.STA         CL_CPI_TYP… 5.0+.0           #> 5        4 FREQUENCY    Dime… FREQ… NA              NA          NA               #> 6        5 TIME_PERIOD  Time… TIME… NA              NA          NA               # }

title: IMF SDMX 3.0 API — sdmx_api
text: Low-level access IMF SDMX 3.0 structure, data, availability, metadata endpoints. functions return API response without converting tidy data frame. See get_data() higher-level interface.

title: Query data availability — sdmx_availability
text: Retrieve available dimension values matching data query without downloading observations.

title: Query data availability — sdmx_availability
code: sdmx_availability(   dataflow,   key = "*",   component_id = "*",   agency_id = "IMF.STA",   version = "+",   context = "dataflow",   filters = list(),   mode = c("exact", "available"),   references = "none",   format = c("json", "xml") )

title: Query data availability — sdmx_availability
text: dataflow Dataflow ID. key SDMX series key. Use "*" filters named filtering. component_id Dimension availability requested. agency_id Maintainer ID. version Dataflow version. context Query context. filters Named list component filters. mode Return exact matches values remain available. references Referenced artefacts include. format Response format. XML responses returned text.

title: Query data availability — sdmx_availability
text: nested list JSON, character value XML, NULL HTTP 204 response.

title: Query statistical data — sdmx_data
text: Retrieve SDMX data message IMF API. raw interface; use get_data() obtain tidy data frame.

title: Query statistical data — sdmx_data
code: sdmx_data(   dataflow,   key = "*",   agency_id = "IMF.STA",   version = "+",   context = "dataflow",   filters = list(),   start_period = NULL,   end_period = NULL,   first_n_obs = NULL,   last_n_obs = NULL,   updated_after = NULL,   include_history = FALSE,   attributes = "dsd",   measures = "all",   format = c("json", "xml") )

title: Query statistical data — sdmx_data
text: dataflow Dataflow ID. key SDMX series key. Use "*" filters named filtering. agency_id Maintainer ID. version Dataflow version. context Query context. filters Named list component filters. start_period, end_period Optional SDMX period bounds. first_n_obs, last_n_obs Optional positive observation limits. used together. updated_after Optional RFC 3339 timestamp. include_history Include historical versions. attributes Attribute detail requested API. measures Measures requested API. format Response format. XML responses returned text.

title: Query statistical data — sdmx_data
text: nested list JSON, character value XML, NULL HTTP 204 response.

title: Query metadata reports — sdmx_metadata
text: Retrieve metadatasets IMF SDMX API.

title: Query metadata reports — sdmx_metadata
code: sdmx_metadata(   provider_id = "*",   metadataset_id = "*",   version = "+",   detail = c("allstubs", "full"),   format = c("json", "xml") )

title: Query metadata reports — sdmx_metadata
text: provider_id Metadata provider ID. metadataset_id Metadataset ID. version Metadataset version. detail Metadata detail level. format Response format. XML responses returned text.

title: Query metadata reports — sdmx_metadata
text: nested list JSON, character value XML, NULL HTTP 204 response.

title: Query structural metadata — sdmx_structure
text: Retrieve IMF SDMX structural metadata dataflows, data structures, codelists, concept schemes.

title: Query structural metadata — sdmx_structure
code: sdmx_structure(   structure_type = "dataflow",   agency_id = "*",   resource_id = "*",   version = "+",   detail = NULL,   references = NULL,   format = c("json", "xml") )

title: Query structural metadata — sdmx_structure
text: structure_type Type structure retrieve. agency_id Maintainer ID. Use "*" maintainers. resource_id Resource ID. Use "*" resources. version Resource version. "+" selects latest stable version. detail Optional SDMX detail level. references Optional referenced artefacts include. format Response format. XML responses returned text.

title: Query structural metadata — sdmx_structure
text: nested list JSON, character value XML, NULL HTTP 204 response.

It implies a survey that experiences a gap for several years prior to the next survey update (this is commonly linked to funding available to run a specific survey (i.e. health surveys), whereas a regular annual survey refers typically to ‘programs’ that are funded regularly and fall under the Statistics Act, and therefore never experience a gap)" #>  #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]] #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$annotations #> list() #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$id #> [1] "OM" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$name #> [1] "Occasional monthly" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$names #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$names$en #> [1] "Occasional monthly" #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$description #> [1] "The event occurs occasionally with an infrequent update that could span from 1 month to several months between events. It implies a survey that experiences a gap for several months prior to the next survey update." #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$descriptions #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[31]]$descriptions$en #> [1] "The event occurs occasionally with an infrequent update that could span from 1 month to several months between events. It implies a survey that experiences a gap for several months prior to the next survey update." #>  #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]] #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$annotations #> list() #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$id #> [1] "_O" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$name #> [1] "Other" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$names #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$names$en #> [1] "Other" #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$description #> [1] "To be used when the qualitative or quantitative values that a variable takes in a data set is associated to multiple occurrences with frequency other than the already defined ones (for example every 5 hours and 32 minutes etc.)" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$descriptions #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[32]]$descriptions$en #> [1] "To be used when the qualitative or quantitative values that a variable takes in a data set is associated to multiple occurrences with frequency other than the already defined ones (for example every 5 hours and 32 minutes etc.)" #>  #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]] #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$annotations #> list() #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$id #> [1] "_U" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$name #> [1] "Unspecified" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$names #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$names$en #> [1] "Unspecified" #>  #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$description #> [1] "To be used when a set of values are reported within a time range but not associated to sub ranges. Often this could happen in case of missing or sparse information. (Let’s say we have two observations for 2020 but we do not know if they are part of a monthly reporting or quarterly reporting)" #>  #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$descriptions #> $data$codelists[[1]]$codes[[33]]$descriptions$en #> [1] "To be used when a set of values are reported within a time range but not associated to sub ranges. title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
text: Configure proxy subsequent IMF API requests current R session. Credentials kept package-private session state, passed httr2::req_proxy(), omitted returned configuration summaries.

title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
code: set_imf_proxy(   url,   port = NULL,   username = NULL,   password = NULL,   auth = proxy_auth_methods )  clear_imf_proxy()

title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
text: url Proxy URL, including scheme hostname. port Optional proxy port. username, password Optional proxy credentials. Supply neither. auth Proxy authentication method supported httr2::req_proxy().

title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
text: redacted summary previous proxy configuration, invisibly.

title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
text: Standard proxy environment variables HTTPS_PROXY, HTTP_PROXY, NO_PROXY continue work package-specific proxy set.

title: Configure an HTTP proxy — set_imf_proxy
code: # Not run: requires a reachable proxy server. The example points at a # placeholder host, so executing it would route a real request through a # non-existent proxy and fail. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set_imf_proxy(   "http://proxy.example.com",   port = 8080,   username = "proxy_user",   password = "proxy_password" )  list_datasets("IMF.STA") clear_imf_proxy() } # }
